Itemized  ·  Compare  ·  USC Verdugo Hills Hospital vs Monterey Park Hospital

USC Verdugo Hills Hospital vs Monterey Park Hospital.

Side-by-side prices for 18 procedures both hospitals publish, plus CMS quality ratings and metro context. Pulled from each hospital's federally-mandated price transparency file. Monterey Park Hospital is cheaper on more procedures (9 vs 8).

USC Verdugo Hills Hospital
Glendale, CA
Keck Medicine of USC
3/5 CMS
Cheaper on 8 of 18 procedures
vs.
Monterey Park Hospital
Monterey Park, CA
AHMC Healthcare
1/5 CMS
Cheaper on 9 of 18 procedures

Head-to-head, by procedure.

Procedure USC Monterey Cheaper
Brain MRI without contrast $3,836 $7,084 USC ↓
Brain MRI with and without contrast $6,870 $7,451 USC ↓
Lumbar spine MRI without contrast $5,343 $7,020 USC ↓
Knee/lower-extremity MRI without contrast $5,469 $7,020 USC ↓
CT abdomen and pelvis with contrast $8,135 $5,114 Monterey ↓
Abdominal ultrasound, complete $2,946 $2,013 Monterey ↓
Mammogram, screening $771 $456 Monterey ↓
Chest X-ray, single view $572 $451 Monterey ↓
Comprehensive metabolic panel $644 $50 Monterey ↓
Lipid panel $239 $23 Monterey ↓
CBC with differential $142 $13 Monterey ↓
TSH (thyroid stimulating hormone) $265 $18 Monterey ↓
Hemoglobin A1c $11 $16 USC ↓
Urinalysis $11 $121 USC ↓
EKG with interpretation — $1,065 tie
Echocardiogram, complete with Doppler $6,767 $3,042 Monterey ↓
Thyroid ultrasound $1,571 $2,132 USC ↓
Carotid duplex ultrasound $2,103 $2,640 USC ↓

Patient experience.

CMS HCAHPS patient survey, period ending 03/31/2025. Bold = higher score.

Measure USC Monterey
HCAHPS overall star rating 3/5 2/5
Would definitely recommend 71% 58%
Hospital rating 9 or 10 of 10 66% 60%
Nurses always communicated well 77% 75%
Doctors always communicated well 77% 71%
Given clear info about recovery 83% 80%
Room and bathroom always clean 76% 73%
Staff always explained meds 60% 62%
Quiet at night, always 41% 47%

Add your insurance.

Cash-pay is one number. With your insurance plan, the actual price differs. Pick your insurer in the comparison tool to see plan-specific rates at both hospitals.

Open comparison →

How to read this comparison.

The cash-pay price is what an uninsured patient would be charged at each hospital. It's the cleanest apples-to-apples comparison because it doesn't depend on your insurance plan.

The CMS rating is the federal quality composite, built from ~50 measures spanning safety, mortality, readmission, patient experience, and timeliness. A 5-star hospital may not be the best at every procedure, and a 3-star hospital can have a strong specific service line. Treat the rating as one input, not the answer.

For your specific insurance plan, prices can shift dramatically. Some hospitals negotiate steep discounts with one insurer and not another. Always check the plan-specific rate before you book.

Data sources: CMS Hospital Price Transparency rule (45 CFR 180.50). CMS Hospital Care Compare (xubh-q36u). CMS HCAHPS patient survey (dgck-syfz). Last refresh: 2026-04-28.
A consumer reading of CMS-mandated MRF data. Not medical or financial advice. Itemized · itemized.health
